title: drypetes caustica
intro: Drypetes caustica is a plant species commonly known as the African poison tree or poison leaf tree, native to tropical regions of West Africa. The plant's leaves contain toxic compounds such as phorbol esters, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Drypetes caustica include its potential to treat various skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its use as a traditional remedy for fever and other ailments. Drypetes caustica leaves can be prepared as a herbal remedy by making a paste or infusion, which is then applied topically or taken orally, depending on the intended use.
